TextENGNew: "Ryuunosuke-san, as I thought--"
changeReason: New translation corrects 'so you decided to' to 'as I thought,' accurately reflecting やっぱり which expresses confirmed suspicion rather than acknowledging a decision.

TextENGNew: When they saw what I was holding, both Miyuki-chan and Towada-san drew a sharp breath.
"Wh-why do you have a gun...?!"
"...Where did you get that?"
changeReason: New translation changes 'What's the deal with that?' to 'Where did you get that?', asking a specific question about origin rather than a vague inquiry, better matching どうしたんですか.

TextENGNew: "No... it's not mine.@k@vS10/62/410101248. ...I'm holding onto it for Sonozaki-san."
changeReason: New translation corrects 'Not sure' to 'No,' accurately reflecting いや as a denial rather than uncertainty.

TextENGNew: I answered flatly under Miyuki-chan's stern gaze.
changeReason: New translation uses 'flatly' for 憮然 instead of 'dejected,' more accurately conveying a curt/stoic demeanor.

TextENGNew: Towada-san peeked at me timidly, her face full of fear.
Well... that was a natural reaction.
"Why would Sonozaki-san have something like this...?"
"I dunno."
"Anyway... what happened with Sonozaki-san?"
"She went to get her car, alone."
"Is that so...?"
"Ryuunosuke-san... are you feeling all right?"
Miyuki-chan stared at the gun in my hand for a while, then turned to me with a concerned expression.
"Huh? What do you mean...?"
"...It's nothing.@k@vS10/40/410300714. If you're feeling all right... I'm glad."
We all returned to our previous seats without saying anything more.
What was Miyuki-chan trying to say...?
"...Can Ryuunosuke-san handle a gun?"
Was that the meaning behind her "Are you feeling all right"...?
I'm fine. I'm familiar with guns! ...Well, from movies and manga anyway.
I gazed down at the gun in my hand.
The safety was still engaged. How do I unlock it...? Well, I'll figure it out somehow.
I considered trying it out... but I stopped. I didn't want to misfire and cause another miserable accident.
Aah... In the end... I'm probably just scared...
Miyuki-chan's concern wasn't unreasonable.
"Is it... real?"
"...Why would she give me a toy at a time like this?"
"...Right."
Otobe anxiously stared at the gun in my hand.
...I wish Sonozaki-san had given him the gun instead. In that case, I'd be half teasing him, half making jokes in a panic...
"So... how far do you think Mion-san is going?"
"I dunno.@k@vS10/62/410101254. She should be back soon. She just went looking for her car."
"Then... why did she leave Arakawa-san with a gun?"
"Well... I have no idea.@k@vS10/62/410101256. She told me I'd be prepared in case something happens..."
"In case something happens? Like what?"
"Don't ask me.@k@vS10/62/410101258. She wouldn't explain anything to me!"
These questions were starting to annoy me, so I stood up with the gun still in hand.
"Where do you think you're going?!"
"...The bathroom!"
I rushed out of the assembly room.
Haah~.
I'd rather not use this thing, if at all possible.
...I mean, is this actually the real thing?
The gun in my hand was starting to feel like just a toy. If I pulled the trigger, a stream of water might just burst out.
......I wanted that to be the truth.
My thoughts continued to focus on Sonozaki-san as I made my way into the cobweb-littered bathroom.
If Sonozaki-san is actually dead, if she's the ghost of a demon that wanders this village...
That's... absurd.
The extreme weight of the gun brought me back to a more realistic train of thought.
This gun was strong evidence against the theory of Mion Sonozaki being a ghost.
If she was the spirit of a demon living in Hinamizawa, she wouldn't have a gun.
I could see a spirit wielding a katana, but not carrying around a gun... Although, I should cast aside my preconceived notions.
Still... if she had a gun this entire time, couldn't she have easily killed people...?
If the people who made our cars disappear tried to make any trouble, she could've simply shot them with this.
Or... was this supposed to be a challenge?
"Try fighting me with that gun.@k@vS10/43/VT3A_mion1002a. I wonder how you'll die? Aahahahaha...!"
Could that be her reason for handing me this gun? ...Ridiculous.
Even after all that thought, I still didn't understand anything.
I took a deep breath and peered through the small bathroom window.
The shrine grounds were pitch black, so I couldn't see anything... at least, that should've been the case.
But contrary to my expectations... there were people... just outside the window.
And not only that... but our eyes met.
They stared at me for a little while, but as soon as I started to move they quickly ducked down and disappeared...
Every drop of blood in my body froze in an instant.
My heart started pounding.
I didn't have the courage to shout, "Who's there?!" at my adversary.
On top of that, my legs were frozen.
Get ahold of yourself.
What are you doing...?! There's someone out there!
I tightly clenched the gun in my hand.
My palm was dripping with so much sweat that the heavy gun was likely to slip out at any moment.
I cautiously peered out the window once more.
...But it looked like nobody was there any more.
But when I gazed into the darkness... the signs were still there.

All I had to go on was the light leaking out from the assembly hall window, but I desperately kept a look out. How long did it take for my eyes to adjust to the darkness outside the window? Five minutes...? Ten...?
In reality, it was probably just a minute or less.
In the shade of the trees... I could make out five or six people.
"...No way?!"
I gently exited the bathroom and headed back to the assembly room, careful not to make any noisy footsteps along the way.
Every floorboard that sank underfoot whittled away at my nerves.
Miyuki-chan and the others were still sitting in the assembly hall.
"Ryuunosuke-san... what's wrong?"
Miyuki-chan called out to me in a dubious voice when she noticed me quietly tiptoe into the room.
No, it might've actually been a perfectly ordinary voice... Miyuki-chan knew nothing of our current situation, so she should've been rather carefree.
"Arakawa-san... do you have an upset stomach?"
"Idiot! That's not what this is!"
Once I realized how loud I was getting, I quieted down in a hurry.
Otobe's relaxed tone irritated me, but it made me realize he was doing nothing more than talking normally.
Calm down... Calm down...
"Are you all right? What is it...?"
"Apparently, it looks like we'll get some use out of this thing after all..."
Then, I quietly told everyone about the people I saw outside the bathroom window.
"I couldn't see very well, but it looked like there were five or six people outside."
"And... you're sure they were actually human?"
"Wha...?!@k@vS10/62/410101267. ...What is that supposed to mean?!"
"If they were the spirits of the dead, coming here to demon away all of us...@k@vS10/41/410200560. then guns would be useless against them, right?"
"Don't be stupid! That's not what this is!"
"What makes you so sure?@k@vS10/41/410200562. You said you didn't get a good look, right?"
"Alright I'll listen. But even then, what can we do?!@k@vS10/62/410101270. Assuming they're actually spirits or whatever, are you suggesting our only option is to lay down and die?!"
"Th-that's not what I'm saying..."
That moment.
Towada-san was sitting with her arms around her knees, quietly muttering something.
"They might have been... the same ones I saw...!"
"J-just a little while ago... I saw them...!@k@vS10/42/410600275. Some dark, mysterious people... close to this shrine...!@k@vS10/42/410600276. A-and then... I-I got scared...!"
Towada-san looked terrified, like all the blood had drained from her face. ...Her eyes were black with dread, and her teeth were chattering.
...Someone telling a lie wouldn't react that way.
"Then... it's settled."
Miyuki-chan reached into her knapsack and pulled out a dark-colored object. Then she quickly rose to her feet and looked down at me as she spoke.
"I'll go check it out.@k@vS10/40/410300721. ...Ryuunosuke-san, can you back me up?"
For a moment, I wasn't sure what she meant... but then I was shocked when I noticed what the "dark object" in her hand truly was.
The thing she was holding... was a gun?!
At first, I thought she'd taken it from me... but then I verified that the gun Sonozaki-san gave me was still in my hands.
Which means... the other gun must belong to Miyuki-chan?!
"Miyuki-chan... what's with that gun...?!"
"I'll explain later.@k@vS10/40/410300723. ...Let's get going."
Miyuki-chan ignored my question and quietly ran toward the entryway with her gun in hand.
I was surprised again by how quickly her body could move.
"Miyuki-chan...@k@vS10/62/410101277. Who... Who on earth are you...?"
Miyuki-chan's gun clicked as she disengaged the safety in the shadow of the doorway.
I was surprised once more by how skillfully her hands moved.
I tried to follow Miyuki-chan's lead and undo my safety... but I couldn't get it off.
"Hand it to me."
Miyuki-chan grabbed the gun from my hand and quickly flipped the safety.
"Here."
"Ah, thanks..."
I grabbed the gun as I said that, and held it tight.
If I pull this trigger, it'll fire a bullet.
A bullet that can kill anything it hits.
Once I realized that simple fact, the weight of the weapon felt even heavier.
Sweat came pouring out of my entire body.
"Arakawa-san...! Are you all right?"
Otobe came rushing toward the entrance.
Miyuki-chan quickly grabbed him by the sleeve and moved her lips close to his ear.
"Wh-what...?"
"You can help too.@k@vS10/40/410300728. ...Alright?"
"M-me...? With what?"
"Otobe-kun, as soon as I open this door I want you to turn on the lights in the shrine grounds.@k@vS10/40/410300730. It's one of those switches, so keep flipping until they're all on."
After saying only that, Miyuki-chan put her hand on the door... and listened carefully.
"Three to the right, four to the left..."
She can piece together the enemy's whereabouts from just that...?
Miyuki-chan's eyes were piercingly sharp... like a wild beast preying on men.
"Miyuki-chan...@k@vS10/62/410101281. What on earth...?"
"We'll talk later!@k@vS10/40/410300733. Please, focus on what's right in front of you.@k@vS10/40/410300734. ...Ryuunosuke-san, let's go!"
Miyuki-chan threw the door open. And that moment,
*bang* *bang* *bang*
The very moment the door came open, I heard sounds reminiscent of exploding firecrackers.
"Uwaaaaah!"
Otobe flipped all the lights in the shrine grounds all at once, then sprinted back into the assembly room at full speed.
The enemies must've been blinded by the lights. They stopped firing for a moment.
Then Miyuki-chan leapt out, charged straight toward a large tree out front, took cover behind it, and quickly turned ready to fire.
"Over there!!"
*crash*
I could hear someone groan "Ugh..." from somewhere off in the distance.
Miyuki-chan continued firing. Another three dry sounds quickly burst out.
*crash* *ka-crash*
The gunshots sounded far more shoddy than they do in movies.
But a thin trail of smoke was emanating from Miyuki-chan's gun.
That's... the power of a gun...
I... couldn't bring myself to move from this spot.
I had no idea where to aim or when to pull the trigger.
No... that's not it. Don't lie to yourself, Ryuunosuke Arakawa.
I didn't have it in me to pull the trigger.
The hand holding the gun... trembled pathetically under the weight of the thing!
*bang*
A bullet whizzed past my nose and struck the door right behind me.
I crouched down and held my arms over my head in a fluster.
*bang* *bang*
The bullets struck the spot where I was just standing, one after another.
"Ryuunosuke-san, run away!@k@vS10/40/410300738. It's not safe here!"
Miyuki-chan shouted at me from behind the tree.
Immediately thereafter, four or five bullets struck the tree Miyuki-chan was hiding behind.
"...!!@k@vS10/40/410300740. Get a load of this, you amateurs!!"
When the rain of bullets ceased, Miyuki-chan leaned out from the back of the big tree and suddenly started firing.
...Suddenly, I started to think.
I began to wonder if I had gotten lost in the world of a movie...?
If these bullets strike me... am I really going to die...?
My paralysis started to clear up as my sense of reality grew weaker.
Thanks to Otobe, the lights in the shrine grounds allowed me to see my enemies.
Men in black suits, running through the darkness...
"Wh-what are these people...?"
I couldn't really make out their faces.
But... I could sense an aura emanating from them, filled with the scum of the earth.
At least they're human... They're not ghosts of Hinamizawa...!
I aimed at one of the men in black... and pulled the trigger!
*pow*
"Ugh?!"
The recoil dealt a heavy blow to my shoulder.
The bullet didn't strike my target, but the man in black took cover behind a nearby tree.
I guess even an amateur like myself can be a threat. As I thought that, I aimed once more at the patch of trees...
"Ryuunosuke-san, look out!"
Miyuki-chan turned back in my direction and shouted.
While I was distracted by the man hiding in the trees, another man in black suddenly jumped out in front of me.
"Die!"
*thud*
I heard a dull sound and looked up to see the man in black collapse as he reached out to me.
And standing behind him was... Sonozaki-san?!
"Wh... what?!"
"...Go hide."
After saying only that, Sonozaki-san ignored the rain of bullets and charged at the men in black with her katana in hand...!
"Fire all you want. You think you'll hit anything?!@k@vS10/43/410400409. Not a chance!!"
Her powerful assertion... was mysteriously proven true. Not a single bullet struck Sonozaki-san.
"Guah?!"
"Gyaaa!!"
Sonozaki-san lifted the handle of her sword up through one man's stomach, then struck the man beside him in the neck.
The men fell to the ground, one after another.
"You're wasting your bullets.@k@vS10/43/410400412. ...Just like I told you!!"
Sonozaki-san dispatched the enemies, one by one.
Her swordplay was as accurate as her footwork. ...And quick, too. ...The difference in strength was huge.
So much so, that it was easy to forget she was fighting to the death... Her combat technique was...
beautiful...
and overwhelming...!!
A man hiding behind a tree pointed his gun toward Sonozaki-san and carefully pulled the trigger.
"Sonozaki-san... watch out!"
"...Too slow!!"
*bang*
"Gaah?!"
The gun flew out of the hands of the man behind the tree. He looked on with terror in his eyes.
I could see the smoke rising from Miyuki-chan's gun.
"Miyuki-chan...?!"
Finally sensing the difference in strength, the men in black retreated while supporting their wounded companions...
Sonozaki-san returned her sword to its sheath, then started heading back to the assembly hall.
Miyuki-chan started heading back too, with her smoking gun still in hand.
Near the entrance, Sonozaki-san turned to Miyuki-chan and grinned.
"...You're pretty good."
"Getting a compliment from an enemy doesn't make me happy."
Miyuki-chan responded tersely to Sonozaki-san's commendation, then abruptly turned her back.
And then, the two of them left it at that and entered the assembly hall.
I followed behind them... but stopped when I noticed something.
Enemy...?
What did she mean by that...?
I stopped in front of the doorway to think it over on my own.
(...Is that it?)
My wandering thoughts seemed to be connected by a single thread.
Mion Sonozaki was the next head of the Sonozaki family... and of course, her father was a high-ranking yakuza officer with roots in the Kanto region.
And the Sonozaki Group was a powerful yakuza organization centered around Hinamizawa and Okinomiya.
I've heard rumors recently that a faction of the Sonozaki Group split off from the Sonozaki family. But people have always misused the Sonozaki name for their own gain. That wasn't anything new.
In other words... this is an internal yakuza conflict.
And the reason Miyuki-chan called Sonozaki-san an "enemy"... was perhaps because she might be in league with the enemy yakuza.
That seemed like a reasonable possibility.
She seemed adept at handling firearms, and had a sharp gaze you'd never see in an ordinary person. So it'd make sense if Miyuki-chan were with the yakuza.
As I returned to the assembly room, Miyuki-chan was wiping her rain-soaked clothes with a cute, floral-patterned hand towel.
I stared at Miyuki-chan's pretty face like a demon stalking its prey, carefully monitoring her movements.
No way... could Miyuki-chan actually be with the yakuza...?
"Are you all right?"
"Huh? Y-yeah..."
"Why don't you dry your clothes too, Ryuunosuke-san?@k@vS10/40/410300746. ...You'll catch a cold if you don't dry off."
"Eh? Ahh, right!"
Not wanting to sit next to Miyuki-chan, I took a seat next to Otobe.
I smiled and tried to keep up appearances.
If Miyuki-chan really was a yakuza member... I had mixed feelings about it.
"That was crazy.@k@vS10/40/410300748. Really surprising, huh...?"
"Ah, yeah. Right.@k@vS10/62/410101295. ...I've never dealt with anything like that before."
Even though I was aware of what was going on, I had no idea how to respond so I quickly threw together a few meaningless words.
This day was full of surprises. I wasn't sure which among them was most surprising.
The feeling of holding a pistol with the safety disengaged, a shootout unfolding right in front of my eyes, the existence of those men in black, Miyuki-chan's true identity...
Everything was far beyond surprising. I was dumbfounded.
"...Ryuunosuke-san, are you all right?"
"Huh? Yeah, yeah, I'm fine!"
Miyuki-chan gave me a dubious look, but she said nothing more.
